hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery becomes safer if the verifier fails when state counters, last verdict pointers, or retained learnings drift away from backlog truth.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` only checked local card structure, so duplicate backlog ids, accepted or rejected count drift, missing last-kept or last-rejected backlog linkage, or retained learning drift could pass as clean systems-optimizer state. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects those state-to-backlog mismatches and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails duplicate or drifting systems-optimizer backlog and state linkages while the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer state must link back to unique backlog ids, verdict counts, and retained learning entries, or recovery can treat stale ratchet history as valid.
